label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The story is about a guy who is born with cancer and knows he might die at any moment. He's been home all his life under his mother's care. One day, he decides to go out and sees a girl at a music store. He gets a CD and starts visiting the store daily, buying CDs so he can interact with her. He's too shy to ask her out until the end, when he leaves his number. After he dies, his mother finds the CDs all with a note from Sophie saying she likes him. The moral is about not waiting to show your feelings. The question is asking which statement is the writer's opinion. The options are A, B, C, D. The story ends with the line: "That's the way life is. Don't wait to show those special people the way you feel. Tomorrow would be too late." So the main message is about not waiting to express your feelings. That seems to match option A: "Don't wait to show your feelings." Option B says "Life always goes like that." The story does say "That's the way life is," but the following sentence emphasizes taking action, so B might be part of it, but the main point is A. Option C is about only having one chance, which is part of the story because the guy dies, but the main message is about expressing feelings, not just the one chance. Option D is about showing
